minor.synopsis: Return the minor component; "" if s is invalid.

returns.doc: minor component; "" if s is invalid

patch.synopsis: Return the patch component; "" if s is invalid.

returns.doc: patch component; "" if s is invalid

prerelease.synopsis: Return the prerelease tail (no leading '-'); "" if absent or invalid.

returns.doc: prerelease tail (no leading '-'); "" if absent or invalid

build.synopsis: Return the build tail (no leading '+'); "" if absent or invalid.

returns.doc: build tail (no leading '+'); "" if absent or invalid
-1 if a<b, 0 if a=b, 1 if a>b; "" if either operand is invalid

compare.description:
Build metadata is ignored per SemVer §10. Prerelease precedence
follows §11.4 (release > prerelease; numeric < alphanumeric).

matches.synopsis: Return 1 iff v satisfies the range expression.

params.name: range
params.doc: range expression (exact, comparator, caret, tilde, AND)

matches.description:
range may be a bare SemVer (exact match), a single comparator
('>'/'<'/'>='/'<='/'='-prefixed), a caret/tilde expansion, or
a space-separated AND of comparators.

STDSNAP.synopsis: m-stdlib — Snapshot testing: serialize an M tree, diff against a baseline.
STDSNAP.description:
Public extrinsics:
  $$serialize^STDSNAP(.data)               — canonical text dump of data tree
  save^STDSNAP(path, .data)                — write serialize(data) to path
  $$matches^STDSNAP(path, .data)           — 1 iff serialize(data) equals file content
  asserts^STDSNAP(.pass, .fail, path, .data, desc) — STDASSERT-style integration

Workflow:
  First run: caller calls save() to capture the baseline snapshot.
  Subsequent runs: caller calls matches() (or asserts()) to compare
  the current data against the saved baseline. Mismatches surface as
  STDASSERT failures with a one-line summary.

Canonical format (line-per-leaf):
  (subscripts)=value
where subscripts is a comma-separated M-syntax list (numeric
subscripts unquoted; string subscripts double-quoted with embedded
doubles per M convention) and value is the leaf node's M-quoted
scalar. Numeric values emit unquoted; everything else is wrapped in
"..." with internal " doubled.

Lines are emitted in $ORDER walk order — natural M-collation, which
gives numeric sort for numeric subscripts and string sort for the
rest. The walk is deterministic; two calls on the same tree produce
byte-identical output.

Implementation: $QUERY-driven recursive descent. The `data` formal
binds to the caller's array via pass-by-reference; $QUERY(data) walks
descendants via M's natural collation order.

serialize.synopsis: Walk data tree; return the canonical line-per-leaf dump.

params.name: data
params.doc: by-ref local; the tree to serialize

returns.doc: canonical line-per-leaf dump; "" for an empty tree

serialize.description:
Trailing LF is *not* emitted — keeps round-trip clean against
writeFile/readFile, which add and strip a trailing LF.

save.synopsis: Write serialize(data) to path. Overwrites if exists.

params.name: path
params.doc: filesystem path; truncated if it exists

params.name: data
params.doc: by-ref local; the tree to serialize

raises.doc: could not open `path` for write (propagated from STDFS)

matches.synopsis: Return 1 iff serialize(data) equals the file's content.

params.name: path
params.doc: filesystem path to the baseline snapshot

returns.doc: 1 iff current matches baseline; 0 otherwise (incl. missing file)

matches.description:
A missing file returns 0 (no match — first run typically calls
save() to seed the snapshot before relying on matches).

asserts.signature: do asserts^STDSNAP(p, f, path, data, desc)

params.name: path
params.doc: filesystem path to the baseline snapshot

raises.doc: in update mode if the write fails (propagated from STDFS)

asserts.description:
On match: increments p and emits PASS via STDASSERT.
On mismatch: increments f and emits FAIL with a brief diagnostic.
Update mode: when ^STDLIB($job,"stdsnap","update")=1, writes the
current snapshot to `path` and records PASS instead of comparing.
Used by m-cli's `m test --update-snapshots` to regenerate
baselines after an intentional change in test output.

STDSTR.synopsis: m-stdlib — String helpers (pad / trim / split / replaceAll / case / repeat).
STDSTR.description:
Public extrinsics:
  $$pad^STDSTR(s,n,c?)         — alias for padLeft (numeric formatting default)
  $$padLeft^STDSTR(s,n,c?)     — left-pad s to width n with c (default " ")
  $$padRight^STDSTR(s,n,c?)    — right-pad s to width n with c (default " ")
  $$trim^STDSTR(s)             — strip leading and trailing whitespace
  $$trimLeft^STDSTR(s)         — strip leading whitespace only
  $$trimRight^STDSTR(s)        — strip trailing whitespace only
  $$replaceAll^STDSTR(s,find,repl) — replace every non-overlapping occurrence
  $$split^STDSTR(s,sep,.out)   — split on sep; populate out(1..N); return N
  $$startsWith^STDSTR(s,prefix) — predicate: does s begin with prefix?
  $$endsWith^STDSTR(s,suffix)  — predicate: does s end with suffix?
  $$toLowerASCII^STDSTR(s)     — A-Z → a-z (ASCII only; preserves non-alpha)
  $$toUpperASCII^STDSTR(s)     — a-z → A-Z (ASCII only; preserves non-alpha)
  $$repeat^STDSTR(s,n)         — concatenate s with itself n times

Whitespace for trim/trimLeft/trimRight is the four ASCII characters
space ($C(32)), tab ($C(9)), LF ($C(10)), CR ($C(13)). Unicode
whitespace classes (NBSP, ideographic space, etc.) are deliberately
not stripped — keeps trim() byte-faithful and idempotent under any
$ZCHSET mode.

Pure-M throughout: $translate, $piece, $find, $extract, $length —
no STDREGEX dep, no $Z* extensions. Runs unchanged on YDB and IRIS.

pad.synopsis: Alias for padLeft — common numeric-formatting shorthand.

params.name: c
params.doc: fill character (default " "; first char only used)

returns.doc: s left-padded with c to width n

padLeft.synopsis: Left-pad s to width n with c (default " "). Returns s unchanged

params.name: c
params.doc: fill character (default " "; first char only used)

returns.doc: s left-padded; s unchanged if $LENGTH(s) >= n

padLeft.description:
if $LENGTH(s) ≥ n. c may be multi-char; pad is built by char-wise
replication of $EXTRACT(c,1).

padRight.synopsis: Right-pad s to width n with c (default " "). Returns s unchanged

params.name: c
params.doc: fill character (default " "; first char only used)

returns.doc: s right-padded; s unchanged if $LENGTH(s) >= n

trim.synopsis: Strip leading and trailing whitespace (space / tab / LF / CR).
trim.description: Internal whitespace is preserved verbatim. Empty input returns "".

returns.doc: s with every non-overlapping match replaced

replaceAll.description:
An empty `find` returns s unchanged (no infinite loop). Replacement
is non-recursive — the new bytes inserted by `repl` are not rescanned.
Implementation: $piece-based join — split s on find, rejoin with repl.

split.synopsis: Split s on sep; populate out(1..N); return N.

params.name: out
params.doc: by-ref local; killed then populated as out(1..N)

returns.doc: number of pieces (0 if s="" or sep="")

split.description: Trailing separator yields a trailing empty element; "a,b," → 3 pieces.

startsWith.synopsis: Return 1 iff s begins with prefix; else 0. Empty prefix → 1.

returns.doc: 1 iff s begins with prefix; empty prefix returns 1

endsWith.synopsis: Return 1 iff s ends with suffix; else 0. Empty suffix → 1.

returns.doc: 1 iff s ends with suffix; empty suffix returns 1

toLowerASCII.synopsis: A-Z → a-z; preserves all other characters.

returns.doc: s with A-Z mapped to a-z (other chars unchanged)

toLowerASCII.description:
Operates byte-wise — no locale awareness, no Unicode handling.
For full Unicode case folding wait on a future STDUNICODE.

toUpperASCII.synopsis: a-z → A-Z; preserves all other characters.

returns.doc: s with a-z mapped to A-Z (other chars unchanged)

toUpperASCII.description: Operates byte-wise — no locale awareness, no Unicode handling.

repeat.synopsis: Concatenate s with itself n times. Returns "" for n ≤ 0 or s="".

returns.doc: s concatenated with itself n times

STDTOML.synopsis: m-stdlib — TOML 1.0 parser (deliberately narrow v1 subset).
STDTOML.description:
Public extrinsics:
  $$parse^STDTOML(text,.root) — parse TOML doc into root tree; return 1/0
  $$valid^STDTOML(text)       — predicate: 1 iff parse succeeds
  $$get^STDTOML(.root,key)    — value lookup; "section.key" addresses tables
  $$type^STDTOML(.root,key)   — "string" / "integer" / "float" / "bool" / ""

Tree representation:
  root("v",path)              — value (the M scalar after coercion)
  root("t",path)              — type tag ("string"/"integer"/"float"/"bool")
  path is the dotted address: "k" for top-level, "section.k" for sectioned.

Grammar (TOML 1.0 subset shipped in v1):
  <doc>        ::= (<line> NL)*
  <line>       ::= <blank> | <comment> | <table> | <pair>
  <comment>    ::= "#" .* (whole-line or trailing — stripped before pair parse)
  <table>      ::= "[" <bare-key> "]"
  <pair>       ::= <bare-key> "=" <value>
  <bare-key>   ::= [A-Za-z0-9_-]+
  <value>      ::= <basic-string> | <integer> | <float> | <bool>
  <basic-string> ::= '"' (chars with \n \t \r \" \\ escapes) '"'
  <integer>    ::= "-"? [0-9]+
  <float>      ::= "-"? [0-9]+ "." [0-9]+
  <bool>       ::= "true" | "false"

Out of scope (queued for v0.x.y under T18):
  - arrays, inline tables, dotted keys, [[array-of-tables]]
  - literal strings ('...'), multi-line strings ("""..." or '''...''')
  - integer literals with underscores, hex/oct/bin int prefixes
  - special floats (inf, -inf, nan)
  - exponent notation in floats (1.5e3)
  - datetime values (TOML offset / local datetime / local date / local time)

parse.synopsis: Parse TOML text into root tree; return 1 on success, 0 on parse error.

params.name: text
params.doc: TOML 1.0 document (subset documented in routine header)

parse.description:
On failure, root is left in whatever partial state the parse
achieved (caller may want to KILL it).

valid.synopsis: Return 1 iff text parses as valid TOML; else 0.
valid.description: Same as parse() but discards the resulting tree.

get.synopsis: Return the value at key (dotted path); "" if absent.

params.name: key
params.doc: dotted path: "k" for top-level; "section.k" for sectioned

returns.doc: the scalar value; "" if key is absent

type.synopsis: Return the type tag at key, or "" if absent.

STDURL.synopsis: m-stdlib — RFC 3986 URI parser, builder, encoder, resolver.
STDURL.description:
Seven public extrinsics:
  $$parse^STDURL(url,.parts)     — split URL into components; → 1/0
  $$build^STDURL(.parts)         — assemble URL from components
  $$encode^STDURL(s,safe)        — percent-encode (RFC 3986 §2.1)
  $$decode^STDURL(s)             — percent-decode (lenient — leaves
                                   malformed % as literal text)
  $$valid^STDURL(url)            — RFC 3986 well-formedness predicate
  $$normalize^STDURL(url)        — case + percent + dot-segment normalization
  $$resolve^STDURL(base,ref)     — resolve a relative reference (§5.3)

The .parts array is a flat namespace with these keys:
  parts("scheme")     parts("userinfo")   parts("host")
  parts("port")       parts("path")       parts("query")
  parts("fragment")
parse() initialises every key (empty when absent) so callers can
index without $get.

Strict-mode resolve: a same-scheme reference like "http:g" against
base "http://a/b/c/d;p?q" resolves to "http:g" (RFC 3986 §5.3
strict; non-strict back-compat is not implemented).

valid() is the strict gate. decode() is intentionally lenient
(matches Python urllib.parse.unquote) — bad %-sequences pass through
as literal text. Use valid() first if strict input is required.

Input is treated as a string of bytes (one M character per byte —
values 0..255 via $ASCII / $CHAR). RFC 3986 §2.5 specifies UTF-8
for non-ASCII; producers/consumers should percent-encode UTF-8
bytes before passing to encode().

params.name: parts
params.doc: by-ref local; killed then populated with 7 keys

parse.description:
All seven keys (scheme/userinfo/host/port/path/query/fragment)
are written; absent components are set to "". Empty input is
valid and yields all-empty parts.

build.synopsis: Reassemble parts into a URL string.
build.description:
Emits authority ("//user@host:port") iff any of userinfo, host,
or port is non-empty. The result round-trips parse() for
canonical inputs.

encode.synopsis: Percent-encode s. Unreserved chars + chars in safe pass through.

params.name: s
params.doc: input string (one M character per byte)

encode.description:
Unreserved set is ALPHA / DIGIT / "-" / "." / "_" / "~"
(RFC 3986 §2.3). Space becomes %20 (per RFC; not "+", which
is the application/x-www-form-urlencoded convention).

decode.synopsis: Percent-decode all valid %HH; leave malformed % as literal.
decode.description:
Use valid() first if strict input is required. "+" is treated
as a literal "+" (form-encoding semantics belong to the caller).

valid.synopsis: True iff url is a well-formed RFC 3986 URI (or relative reference).
valid.description:
Empty string is valid. Rejects raw spaces, control characters,
and malformed %HH.

normalize.description:
Lowercases scheme + host, uppercases %HH hex digits,
percent-decodes unreserved characters, and removes
dot-segments from the path.

resolve.synopsis: Resolve ref against base per RFC 3986 §5.3 (strict mode).
resolve.description:
Strict mode: a reference that begins with the same scheme
as base is still treated as scheme-bearing.

STDUUID.description:
Five public extrinsics:
  $$v4^STDUUID()         — random UUID v4 ("xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx")
  $$v7^STDUUID()         — time-ordered UUID v7
  $$valid^STDUUID(u)     — 1 if u is canonical 36-char hex form
  $$version^STDUUID(u)   — integer 1..15 from the version nibble, "" if invalid
  $$variant^STDUUID(u)   — "ncs" | "rfc4122" | "microsoft" | "future" | ""

v4 randomness is from $RANDOM (Mersenne Twister) — NOT cryptographically
strong. Adequate for distributed primary keys; do not use for tokens.
v7 timestamp is ms since 1970-01-01 UTC (48 bits): high 48 bits encode
the timestamp so byte-wise sort = generation order.

All output is lowercase hex per RFC 9562 §4 recommendation.

v4.synopsis: Return a new RFC-4122 v4 UUID.
v4.description:
122 bits of randomness; version nibble='4'; variant nibble in 8/9/a/b.
Randomness is from $RANDOM (Mersenne Twister) — adequate for distributed
primary keys; not cryptographically strong (do not use for tokens).

v7.synopsis: Return a new RFC-9562 v7 UUID (time-ordered).

returns.doc: canonical 36-char hex UUID v7; byte-wise sort = generation order

examples: set id=$$v7^STDUUID()  ; sorts in generation order
v7.description:
First 48 bits = ms-since-Unix-epoch (sortable). 12-bit rand_a,
variant nibble (8/9/a/b), 12-bit rand_b, 50-bit rand_c.

valid.synopsis: Return 1 iff u is a canonical 36-char hex UUID; else 0.
valid.description:
Accepts both lowercase and uppercase hex. Hyphens must sit
at exactly positions 9, 14, 19, 24.

version.synopsis: Return integer version (1..15) from position 15, or "" if invalid.

returns.doc: 1..15 from the version nibble; "" if `u` is not valid

version.description: For a v4 UUID this returns 4; for v7, 7. Empty string for malformed.

variant.synopsis: Classify UUID variant from the high bits of position 20.
variant.description:
"ncs" (high bit 0), "rfc4122" (high 10), "microsoft" (high 110),
"future" (high 111). Empty string for malformed input.

STDXFRM.synopsis: m-stdlib — Higher-order array transforms (map / filter / reduce via @-indirection lambdas).
STDXFRM.description:
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-036
M-MOD-036 flags M's `@` indirection on a "tainted" local — but
XECUTE-via-@ of a caller-supplied expression IS the contract of
this module: the lambda is the abstraction. Same shape as STDMOCK's
`do @resolved@(.args)` and STDFIX's `xecute @cleanupCmd`.

Public entry points:
  do map^STDXFRM(.in, expr, .out)      — out(k) := <expr> for each k
  do filter^STDXFRM(.in, expr, .out)   — copy in(k)→out(k) iff <expr>
  $$reduce^STDXFRM(.in, expr, init)    — fold left; <expr> is new acc

The lambda string `expr` is evaluated via `@expr` in this module's
stack frame, so it sees these locals:
  value  — the current element's value (in(k))
  key    — the current subscript (k)
  acc    — (reduce only) the accumulator carried forward

Walk discipline:
  - $ORDER-walk at depth 1 only (the canonical "1-D vector" shape).
  - Subscript shape doesn't matter — int, string, sparse all work.
  - For map/filter, `out` is killed before the walk so stale
    leftovers from a prior call do not leak through.
  - For reduce, the empty-input case returns `init` unchanged.

Error semantics: if `expr` raises (compile error, $ECODE set,
division by zero, etc.) the error propagates to the caller's
$ETRAP unmodified. STDXFRM does not catch.

Pure-M throughout — no $Z* extensions. Runs unchanged on YDB and
IRIS. The @-indirection idiom is ANSI standard since the M[UMPS]
X11.1 standard.

params.name: out
params.doc: by-ref local; killed then populated as out(k)

examples: new a,out  set a(1)=1,a(2)=2,a(3)=3  do map^STDXFRM(.a,"value*2",.out)
map.description: Lambda locals visible to expr: `value` (in(k)) and `key` (k).

params.name: out
params.doc: by-ref local; killed then populated with kept entries

filter.description: Subscripts are preserved. Values are copied verbatim.

reduce.synopsis: Fold left: walk in, evaluate expr with `acc`+`value`+`key`.

STDXML.synopsis: m-stdlib — XML parser (well-formed XML 1.0 subset, in-progress).
STDXML.description:
m-lint: disable-file=M-MOD-036
M-MOD-036 flags M's `@` indirection on a "tainted" local. The
XPath helpers (T27) build subscript references from path strings
that are 100% generated from internal `for i=1:1:childCount` loops
— no user input flows into the indirection target. The pattern is
documented in `docs/modules/stdxml.md` "T27 path-walk indirection".

Public extrinsics (v0):
  $$parse^STDXML(text,.root)              — parse text into root tree; 1/0
  $$valid^STDXML(text)                    — predicate
  $$rootName^STDXML(.node)                — element tag name
  $$attr^STDXML(.node, name)              — attribute value or ""
  $$text^STDXML(.node)                    — direct text content
  $$childCount^STDXML(.node)              — count of element children
  $$childByName^STDXML(.node, name, .out) — first child with name → .out; 1/0
  $$lastError^STDXML()                    — diagnostic string or ""

Tree shape (caller-owned; pass by reference):
  node("name")           — element tag
  node("attr", attrName) — attribute value (decoded)
  node("text")           — direct text content (decoded; concatenated text nodes)
  node("childCount")     — number of element children
  node("child", n)       — n-th child element (recursive structure)

Child traversal MUST go through `childByName` (or analogous helpers
that internally `merge` the child subtree to a non-subscripted local).
Direct passing of `.node("child", n)` is **invalid YDB syntax** —
pass-by-reference of a subscripted local is disallowed at compile
time (TOOLCHAIN-FINDINGS row 2026-05-06, demoted to docs 2026-05-07).
The merge-then-pass idiom is canonical for any STDXML descent.

Grammar (v0 subset of XML 1.0):
  <document>   ::= <ws>? <element> <ws>?
  <element>    ::= <empty-tag> | <stag> <content> <etag>
  <empty-tag>  ::= "<" <name> <attrs>? <ws>? "/>"
  <stag>       ::= "<" <name> <attrs>? <ws>? ">"
  <etag>       ::= "</" <name> <ws>? ">"
  <attrs>      ::= ( <ws> <attr> )+
  <attr>       ::= <name> <ws>? "=" <ws>? <attval>
  <attval>     ::= '"' <chardata> '"' | "'" <chardata> "'"
  <content>    ::= <chardata>? ( <element> <chardata>? )*
  <name>       ::= [A-Za-z_:] [A-Za-z0-9_:.-]*
  <chardata>   ::= text with the 5 standard entities decoded

T26 — DTDs / DOCTYPE / internal subset / custom entities (closed
2026-05-08): `<!DOCTYPE root [...]>` is consumed at the prolog;
`<!ENTITY name "value">` declarations populate ctx("entity",name)
and expand in subsequent text + attribute content. `<!ELEMENT>` /
`<!ATTLIST>` / `<!NOTATION>` decls are parsed but not enforced
(skipped through the next `>`). External DTDs (`SYSTEM "url"`,
`PUBLIC "id" "url"`) are accepted in the prolog and silently
ignored — only the internal subset is materialised. Parameter
entities (`%name;`) and external entity references are out of
scope; if a real consumer needs them, lift through a follow-up.

parse.synopsis: Parse text into root tree; return 1/0.
parse.description: Sets ^STDLIB($job,"stdxml","err") with a diagnostic on failure.

valid.synopsis: Return 1 iff text parses as valid XML; else 0.

rootName.synopsis: Return the element tag name; "" if missing.

ns.synopsis: Return the namespace URI for the element; "" if not in any namespace.

returns.doc: namespace URI; "" if element is not in any namespace

ns.description: T25 — uses xmlns / xmlns:prefix declarations in scope.

attrNs.synopsis: Return the namespace URI for an attribute; "" if unprefixed or absent.

returns.doc: namespace URI; "" if unprefixed or absent

attrNs.description:
Per XML Namespaces 1.0 §6.2, default xmlns does NOT apply to
unprefixed attributes; only prefixed attrs carry a namespace URI.

xpath.synopsis: Run an XPath query; populate results(1..N); return N.

params.name: results
params.doc: by-ref local; killed then populated as results(1..N)

returns.doc: match count; 0 for an unparseable expression

xpath.description:
Supports element paths, absolute paths, descendant axis (`//`),
position predicates, wildcards (`*` / `@*`), attribute axis
(`@attr`), and predicate expressions with comparison operators
and functions (position, last, name, text, count, etc.).

params.name: out
params.doc: by-ref local; merged with first match

returns.doc: 1 iff at least one match; 0 otherwise

xpathText.synopsis: Return the direct text of the first match; "" if none.

returns.doc: direct text of first match; "" if no match

text.synopsis: Return direct text content; "" if no text.

returns.doc: direct text content; "" if no text

childCount.synopsis: Return number of element children; 0 if none.

childByName.synopsis: Find first child with `name`; merge into `.out`. 1/0.

params.name: out
params.doc: by-ref local; killed then populated with the child subtree

childByName.description:
Pass-by-ref of `.out` allows the caller to receive the child
subtree without violating YDB's `.x(SUBS)` syntax limit.

lastError.synopsis: Return the last parse error diagnostic; "" if none / parse succeeded.

returns.doc: last diagnostic; "" if last parse succeeded
